# iaas_ssh_key (Resource)

Manages an SSH public key in your IaaS account. SSH keys can be injected into instances at provision time.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "iaas_ssh_key" "example" {
  name = "laptop"

  # The SSH public key material. Changing this forces a new resource.
  public_key = "ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAILcl0K3kLJv6N4F2bWj2vJxQ1q3qY8m0Q3a2bC4d5E6F you@example.com"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Display name for the SSH key. Max 32 characters; only lowercase letters, digits, spaces, dots, and hyphens are allowed (case-insensitive). Validated server-side.
- `public_key` (String) The SSH public key material (e.g. an ssh-ed25519 or ssh-rsa line). Cannot be changed after creation; modifying it forces replacement.

### Read-Only

- `comments` (String) Comment associated with the key, derived by the API from the key's natural trailing comment. Server-managed.
- `fingerprint` (String) Fingerprint of the public key, computed by the API.
- `id` (String) UUID of the SSH key, assigned by the API.
